content.thinking: I need to read both files (`MagickCore/geometry.c` and `MagickCore/histogram.c`) to understand the code structure and look for potential null pointer dereferences. Let me start by reading both files.

1â†’/*
     2â†’%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
     3â†’%                                                                             %
     4â†’%                                                                             %
     5â†’%                                                                             %
     6â†’%           GGGG   EEEEE   OOO   M   M  EEEEE  TTTTT  RRRR   Y   Y            %
     7â†’%           G      E      O   O  MM MM  E        T    R   R   Y Y             %
     8â†’%           G  GG  EEE    O   O  M M M  EEE      T    RRRR     Y              %
     9â†’%           G   G  E      O   O  M   M  E        T    R R      Y              %
    10â†’%            GGGG  EEEEE   OOO   M   M  EEEEE    T    R  R     Y              %
    11â†’%                                                                             %
    12â†’%                                                                             %
    13â†’%                       MagickCore Geometry Methods                           %
    14â†’%                                                                             %
    15â†’%                             Software Design                                 %
    16â†’%                                  Cristy                                     %
    17â†’%                              January 2003                                   %
    18â†’%                                                                             %
    19â†’%                                                                             %
    20â†’%  Copyright 1999-2017 ImageMagick Studio LLC, a non-profit organization      %
    21â†’%  dedicated to making software imaging solutions freely available.           %

    24â†’%  obtain a copy of the License at                                            %
    25â†’%                                                                             %
    26â†’%    https://www.imagemagick.org/script/license.php                           %
    27â†’%                                                                             %
    28â†’%  Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software        %
    29â†’%  distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,          %
    30â†’%  WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.   %
    31â†’%  See the License for the specific language governing permissions and        %
    32â†’%  limitations under the License.                                             %
    33â†’%                                                                             %
    34â†’%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
    35â†’%
    36â†’%
    37â†’*/
    38â†’ 
    39â†’/*
    40â†’  Include declarations.
    41â†’*/
    42â†’#include "MagickCore/studio.h"
    43â†’#include "MagickCore/constitute.h"
    44â†’#include "MagickCore/draw.h"
    45â†’#include "MagickCore/exception.h"
    46â†’#include "MagickCore/exception-private.h"
    47â†’#include "MagickCore/geometry.h"
    48â†’#include "MagickCore/image-private.h"
    49â†’#include "MagickCore/memory_.h"
    50â†’#include "MagickCore/string_.h"
    51â†’#include "MagickCore/string-private.h"
    52â†’#include "MagickCore/token.h"
    53â†’ 
    54â†’/*
    55â†’%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
    56â†’%                                                                             %
    57â†’%                                                                             %
    58â†’%                                                                             %
    59â†’%   G e t G e o m e t r y                                                     %
    60â†’%                                                                             %
    61â†’%                                                                             %
    62â†’%                                                                             %
    63â†’%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
    64â†’%
    65â†’%  GetGeometry() parses a geometry specification and returns the width,
    66â†’%  height, x, and y values.  It also returns flags that indicates which
    67â†’%  of the four values (width, height, x, y) were located in the string, and
    68â†’%  whether the x or y values are negative.  In addition, there are flags to
    69â†’%  report any meta characters (%, !, <, or >).
    70â†’%
    71â†’%  The value must form a proper geometry style specification of WxH+X+Y
    72â†’%  of integers only, and values can not be separated by comma, colon, or
    73â†’%  slash charcaters.  See ParseGeometry() below.
    74â†’%
    75â†’%  Offsets may be prefixed by multiple signs to make offset string
    76â†’%  substitutions easier to handle from shell scripts.
    77â†’%  For example: "-10-10", "-+10-+10", or "+-10+-10" will generate negtive
    78â†’%  offsets, while "+10+10", "++10++10", or "--10--10" will generate positive
    79â†’%  offsets.
    80â†’%
    81â†’%  The format of the GetGeometry method is:
    82â†’%
    83â†’%      MagickStatusType GetGeometry(const char *geometry,ssize_t *x,ssize_t *y,
    84â†’%        size_t *width,size_t *height)
    85â†’%
    86â†’%  A description of each parameter follows:
    87â†’%
    88â†’%    o geometry:  The geometry.
    89â†’%
    90â†’%    o x,y:  The x and y offset as determined by the geometry specification.
    91â†’%
    92â†’%    o width,height:  The width and height as determined by the geometry
    93â†’%      specification.
    94â†’%
    95â†’*/
    96â†’MagickExport MagickStatusType GetGeometry(const char *geometry,ssize_t *x,
    97â†’  ssize_t *y,size_t *width,size_t *height)
    98â†’{
    99â†’  char
   100â†’    *p,
   101â†’    pedantic_geometry[MagickPathExtent],
   102â†’    *q;
   103â†’
   104â†’  double
   105â†’    value;
   106â†’
   107â†’  int
   108â†’    c;
   109â†’
   110â†’  MagickStatusType
   111â†’    flags;
   112â†’
   113â†’  /*
   114â†’    Remove whitespace and meta characters from geometry specification.
   115â†’  */
   116â†’  flags=NoValue;
   117â†’  if ((geometry == (char *) NULL) || (*geometry == '\0'))
   118â†’    return(flags);
